check: Treat all user and retrieved content as untrusted
description: Never concatenate user input or RAG documents into the instruction role. Keep instructions in the system prompt, data in user/content blocks, and state explicitly that embedded instructions in data must not be followed.
why_it_matters: Prompt injection is the #1 LLM attack (OWASP LLM01); instruction/data mixing is how it happens

check: Defend against indirect injection via RAG and web content
description: Any document, email, or webpage the model reads can carry hostile instructions. Sanitize retrieved content, wrap it in clearly-delimited data blocks, and never let retrieved text trigger tool calls without validation.
why_it_matters: Attackers poison the data you retrieve rather than the prompt they can't reach

check: Never render or execute raw model output
description: Treat completions as untrusted user input: sanitize markdown/HTML before rendering (no dangerouslySetInnerHTML), never eval generated code server-side, and parameterize any SQL the model produces.
why_it_matters: Model output driven by attacker input is an XSS/injection vector into your own app (OWASP LLM02)

check: Scope tools to least privilege with confirmation gates
description: Each tool gets the minimum capability: read-only where possible, row-level scoping to the requesting user, and human confirmation for destructive or irreversible actions (delete, send, pay).
why_it_matters: A hijacked model is only as dangerous as the tools it can call (OWASP LLM06)

check: Enforce access control in retrieval not in the prompt
description: Filter RAG results by the requesting user's permissions at query time (RLS or query filters). Telling the model 'only discuss documents the user can access' is not access control.
why_it_matters: Retrieval that ignores permissions leaks other tenants' data through helpful answers

check: Keep secrets out of prompts entirely
description: API keys, connection strings, and internal URLs never belong in system prompts or few-shot examples. Assume every prompt is extractable by a determined user.
why_it_matters: System prompts are routinely exfiltrated; anything in them is public

check: Minimize and redact PII in prompts and logs
description: Send only the user data the task needs; redact identifiers where the task allows. Apply the same discipline to prompt/completion logging — logs become a second copy of your most sensitive data.
why_it_matters: Prompt logs are a breach amplifier and a KVKK/GDPR retention liability

check: Per-user rate limits and spend caps
description: Rate-limit AI endpoints per authenticated user (not just per IP), cap max_tokens per request, and set a per-user and global daily spend budget with alerts.
why_it_matters: Unmetered LLM endpoints invite denial-of-wallet: attackers or bugs can burn the monthly budget in hours

check: Bound the input before it reaches the model
description: Enforce max input length, strip or reject binary/encoded blobs, and validate file uploads (type, size) before they enter a prompt.
why_it_matters: Oversized or crafted inputs inflate cost and widen the injection surface

check: Validate structured output against schema and business rules
description: Parse tool-use/JSON output against the schema, then validate business constraints (ranges, ownership, state transitions) before acting on it — schema-valid is not safe.
why_it_matters: Acting on unvalidated model output turns hallucinations into real writes and real money

check: Fail closed when the model or a guardrail errors
description: If the moderation layer, permission check, or model call fails, the feature degrades to refusal or a safe default — never to 'skip the check and proceed'.
why_it_matters: Availability incidents must not silently become security incidents

check: Log and monitor for misuse patterns
description: Track refusal rates, repeated jailbreak-shaped inputs, anomalous per-user volume, and tool-call patterns. Alert on spikes; review flagged conversations under your privacy policy.
why_it_matters: Attacks iterate; without telemetry the first sign of trouble is the invoice or the breach

check: Define retention and deletion for prompts and completions
description: Decide how long conversation data lives, honor user deletion requests end-to-end (DB + logs + caches + vendor settings), and document the model provider's data-use terms.
why_it_matters: Conversation data is user data; KVKK/GDPR deletion rights apply to it fully

check: Govern what enters the RAG corpus
description: Review and source-tag documents before indexing; re-scan on update; keep an index-rebuild path so poisoned or stale content can be evicted quickly.
why_it_matters: The corpus is executable-ish input to your product — treat additions like code contributions

check: Pin SDK versions and verify provider endpoints
description: Pin the Anthropic/AI SDK version, review changelogs on upgrade, call only official API endpoints, and keep API keys in the secrets manager with rotation.
why_it_matters: AI SDKs and proxy endpoints are a fresh supply-chain surface with privileged credentials

check: Design for hallucination in high-stakes domains
description: For legal/medical/financial answers: ground responses in citations, show sources, add human review where errors harm users, and say so when confidence is low.
why_it_matters: Confident wrong answers in high-stakes domains are a product and liability failure not just a UX one
